text:
There was an old bear who loved cheese. He’d go onto people’s porches every night, snarf down their leftover cheese wheels, and leave a tiny pile of paw prints.

One evening, instead of cheese, a rabbit left a big, smelly scarecrow on his porch. The bear just nodded quietly, picked up the scarecrow, and made a little pile of cotton outdoors. The next morning, the scarecrow stood proudly, and the bear examined it from afar. A few weeks later, they exchanged a polite bow.

That bear lived a long life, minding his own business until nature called—he couldn’t resist a visit to that nice-looking swamp. He ended up losing an entire limb. When medics arrived, they found him reading a book about owl behavior.

The campers loved the scarecrow. They named him Orpheus. They liked him so much they never took him out hunting. Every evening they’d watch Orpheus from their canoe, chuckling at
